summary.intent: Add Slack as a channel option in the onboarding flow, which was missing despite being available in the main channels page.
summary.outcome: Slack was fully added to onboarding: ChannelTypeSelector updated, SlackForm component created, form wired into ChannelStep, changes committed and pushed.

repo: Onboarding channel forms use useState pattern (not Preact signals like page-channels.js modals)
ChannelTypeSelector uses a grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 layout that naturally accommodates 7 items in a 3rd row
icon-slack and channelDisplayLabel('slack') already existed; Slack was in channels_offered but lacked an onboarding form
The AddSlackModal in page-channels.js serves as the source-of-truth template for Slack form fields when adapting to onboarding

code.finding: ChannelTypeSelector options array defines [type, icon-class, label] tuples; Slack inserted between Discord and Matrix
AddSlackModal contains the canonical Slack form fields: Account ID, Bot Token, Connection Mode, conditional App Token/Signing Secret, DM Policy, user allowlist, advanced config patch

workflow: Run biome check --write after JS changes to lint/format
E2E onboarding spec tests CSS rendering via synthetic DOM, not actual channel list — no update needed when adding channels
Branch was behind remote; required stash + pull --rebase + restore before committing

friction: Branch was behind remote by 1 commit, requiring stash/rebase dance before push
Multiple reads of the same file sections were needed to locate insertion points
